item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was so much worse because I've extensively read tons of strips from the comics you chose. I could perfectly picture the strip the way you described it.
That last note gave me such perfect creeps. I feel like I almost understand what this SCP is, but it's just a little too far…
+1 all the way

item.title: Great Job!
item.content:encoded: This is one of my new favorites SCPs right now. The concept of the SCP is really interesting, really well written, keep up the good work!

item.title: no oh god why
item.content:encoded: You hit something very close to home with me and evoked a high sense of unease and terror. i used to shit my pants from Creepypastas like this and the bit with Bloom County really hit the nail on the head because i love that strip and i got to imagining a hideous amalgamation of all those characters speaking to me and that was terrifying. terrific stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just filled in the blanks in the original Extranormal Events entry, then picked papers at random. That's one freaky coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I loved it.
Secondly, why the hell is one of the newspapers in the town I live in.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: My edits should address the mobile issue, and most of your grammatical critiques. Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Westhead Media is a thoroughly unpleasant GOI.

This was a good addition to the canon. The dismissive 'champ' works well to convey the casual brutality. Like the saying goes, people who address you as 'my friend' aren't your friends.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Here Be Crit / Opinion
ByeBye

Hey Nykacolaquantum,

Nitpicks:

Webcrawler S29F87 ("JUMBLE") is to search online for keywords pertaining to SCP-5042-A bi-hourly, with all results forwarded to Site-28.

The problem with using terms like "bi-hourly" is that it could mean twice an hour or once every two hours; I suggest clarifying.

Upon verification, Mobile Task Force Mu-50 ("Not Me") is to be dispatched immediately, retrieve SCP-5042-A materials and locally disperse amnestics.

This sentence feels a bit clanky. Also "upon verification" of what?

Note: All the sentences in the conprocs start with "are to be" "is to be"; might want to try landing more variety.

SCP-5042 encompasses three interrelated phenomena.

As the first sentence in your description, this is fairly lackluster.

SCP-5042-A collectively refer to…

This is a personal opinion but it would sound better as something like, "SCP-5042-A is the collective designation for…"

Recurring symbolism of eggs being broken, and/or fractures in the sky.

This is both a compliment and a concern: this was the first line that actually piqued my interest.

So Addendum 5042-1, is probably the coolest part of this BUT I had to switch to my laptop halfway through because it's almost unreadable on mobile. It could just be my phone, but it's worth checking out because a lot of users read on mobile.

Addendum 2

This is a consistency thing for me, the first addendum is 5042-1, the second should be formatted the same, 5042-2.

As a second note, this section feels a bit rushed– But the note at the end is well executed and brings it together nicely

Opinion
So all of these nitpicks aside, and despite the beginning being a tad dry, I enjoyed this. Once this picks up it's interesting enough and has enough "wtf?" factor to keep me going. I think this could be refined into something great, but as is it's pretty good. +1



Site crit : Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is… surprisingly something I really enjoyed! Great job here!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Tanhony's Group of Interest Westhead Media intrigued me from the moment I first saw it. I'm happy to finally be able to put out my take on it out there.

Thanks to Tanhony for the feedback they gave.

Credits:
Image is CC-BY 2.0 Knick Banas https://www.flickr.com/photos/idontknowwhat1/2012678616
Extranormal Events entry authored by Ihp
Responsive table code by Woedenaz
